name: phenotype-landing
absorption_note: ARCHIVE_ONLY (rejected ABSORB into phenodocs/packages/landing/). phenotype-landing is a 7-site Astro multi-tenant factory, not a single landing page. Each site (sites/{agileplus,byteport,hwledger,phenokits,projects,thegent}-landing Astro+Bun + sites/odin-landing static HTML) has its own package.json + bun.lock + vercel.json + .github/workflows/ci.yml + vitest.config.ts. The literal `cp -r phenotype-landing/* phenodocs/packages/landing/` would copy 7 independently-deployable Astro sites (each with its own vercel deploy target and CI workflow) into a VitePress federation hub — architecturally mismatched. Tracked source is ~1.5 MB but on-disk footprint is ~1.6 GB (mostly node_modules). Per task failsafe clause (build heavy or conflicts exist), pivoted to ARCHIVE_ONLY: source repo archived on GitHub via `gh repo archive`; no code copied; forward-pointer README at phenodocs/packages/landing/ captures the deferred absorption decision. Parallel precedent: omniroute-rust and PhenoVCS (both ARCHIVE_ONLY 2026-07-17) for the same 'multi-crate/-site workspace vs monolithic absorber' structural mismatch.
note: Audited 2026-07-17; ARCHIVE_ONLY disposition (was ABSORB in pre-audit queue). Absorbed not into phenodocs/packages/landing/ but into phenotype-registry (boundary doc + forward-pointer README): rejected absorption because (a) source is a 7-site Astro monorepo, not a single landing page, (b) each site has its own package.json + vercel.json + CI workflow making it architecturally mismatched with phenodocs's VitePress federation hub, (c) no root package.json means phenodocs's `workspaces: [packages/*]` can't ingest it as a single workspace entry, (d) tracked source 1.5MB but on-disk 1.6GB (node_modules heavy). Boundary doc at docs/boundary/phenotype-landing.md captures the rejected absorption in detail; forward-pointer README at phenodocs/packages/landing/ marks the deferred state. [ABSORBED 2026-07-17]
